---
title: "Collection of Datasets"
output: html_document
---

*** 
### Overview
I initially developed this collection as part of my Introduction to Applied Statistics Class.

*** 
### Datasets in R (1)

```{r message=FALSE,echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!require("DT")) install.packages('DT')
if (!require("plyr")) install.packages('plyr')
library(DT)

d<-read.csv("datalists/R_Packages_data.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE, header=FALSE)
cut=round(nrow(d)/2,digits=0)
d1=d[1:cut,]
d2=d[(cut+1):nrow(d),]
x<-arrange(d1,V1,V2)
m = matrix(as.matrix(x[1:5]),ncol=5)

colnames(m) = c('<span>Package</span>','Dataset','Title','csv','doc')
datatable(m, escape = FALSE)
```

### Datasets in R (2)

```{r message=FALSE,echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!require("DT")) install.packages('DT')
if (!require("plyr")) install.packages('plyr')
library(DT)

x<-arrange(d2,V1,V2)
m = matrix(as.matrix(x[1:5]),ncol=5)

colnames(m) = c('<span>Package</span>','Dataset','Title','csv','doc')
datatable(m, escape = FALSE)
```

### Data Set and Repository Lists

```{r message=FALSE,echo=FALSE,warning=FALSE}

d<-read.csv("datalists/collections_data.csv", header=FALSE, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
#x<-arrange(d,V1)
m = matrix(as.matrix(d), ncol=1)
colnames(m) = c('<span>Name</span>')
datatable(m, escape = FALSE)
```
